primary_metric: Cross-repo contract shape drift fails the consumer at CI time instead of in production (closes the W16 silent-pass class)
success_metrics: scripts/check-contract-fixtures.ts validates required_keys on every vendored fixture record + the live src/data/features/*.json consumer (113 files) — HARD fail on shape drift (T1, measured via 7 unit tests + npm run contract:check)
audit-log canonical sampler reads Upstash Redis, sanitizes each record (sanitizeForPublicExport), asserts required_keys, no-ops without creds (T1, measured via 9 Redis-free unit tests)

Weekly re-sample cron (Mon 07:00 UTC) re-samples audit-log + re-vendors FT2 fixtures and opens a PR on change — closes the 15-day staleness both repos exhibited at 2026-06-22 (T2, verified by first scheduled cron fire)
warn-only CI step on every fitme-story PR runs contract:check; FT2 fixtures re-sampled fresh (T1, PRs merged green)
kill_criteria: Freshness check is too strict and breaks fitme-story CI when an FT2-produced fixture goes stale between syncs — the consumer cannot self-re-sample those
audit-log sampler leaks PII into the committed, publicly-reachable fixture
Consumer tests hand-author the producer shape (the exact anti-pattern that caused the 2026-05-24 W16 incident), so drift still passes silently
kill_criteria_resolution: All 3 mitigated by design. (1) Freshness is ASYMMETRIC: WARN (not hard) for FT2-produced fixtures fitme-story vendors but cannot re-sample; HARD only for fitme-story-owned contracts (audit-log) where it controls re-sampling. Test `stale FT2-produced fixture → WARN (not hard)` enforces. (2) The sampler runs `sanitizeForPublicExport` on every record before write (strips operator_label → hash); test `sanitize-strips-PII` asserts no raw PII survives. (3) The checker validates against the REAL producer-sampled fixture + live src/data/features data, never inline literals; the sampler's own sample-time required_keys assertion catches producer drift before a bad fixture ships. Tests use temp dirs, never hand-authoring the producer shape.
dispatch_pattern: serial (agent-driven, cross-repo: fitme-story consumer + FT2 fixture re-sample)
scope_summary: fitme-story-side consumer adoption + weekly re-sample cadence for F-CONTRACT-FIXTURE-SAMPLING (E-15). The FT2 substrate (scripts/sample-contract-fixtures.py + make check-contract-fixtures, warn-only) shipped 2026-06-07; this closes the cross-repo half. Ships: a unified consumer checker (shape HARD, freshness asymmetric) validating vendored fixtures + the live src/data/features consumer; the audit-log canonical sampler (fitme-story is that contract's canonical producer via Upstash Redis); a weekly re-sample cron; a warn-only CI gate; and a re-sample of the 15-day-stale FT2 fixtures. Promotion of both repos' contract gates warn-only → blocking is DEFERRED to an operator decision (conflicts with FT2 operator decision D2 keeping the FT2 gate drift-only/warn-only, and the day-0 consumer gate needs a §3.5 soak).

tasks.description: scripts/check-contract-fixtures.ts — unified consumer checker; SHAPE (required_keys) HARD, FRESHNESS asymmetric (WARN FT2-vendored / HARD fitme-story-owned); validates vendored fixtures + live src/data/features
scripts/check-contract-fixtures.test.ts — 7 tests, temp-dir based (never hand-authors producer shape)
scripts/sample-audit-log-contract.ts — audit-log canonical sampler (Redis read + sanitize + required_keys assert; no-ops without creds)
scripts/sample-audit-log-contract.test.ts — 9 Redis-free tests (sanitize-strips-PII, drift detection, creds detection)
.github/workflows/contract-resample-weekly.yml — Mon 07:00 UTC; re-sample audit-log + re-vendor FT2 fixtures, PR on change
package.json contract:check script + integrity.yml warn-only CI step
Re-sample 15-day-stale FT2 fixtures (FT2 PR #790) + candidates-doc E-15 status note
Promote both repos' contract gates warn-only → blocking
tasks.deferred_reason: Operator decision — conflicts with FT2 operator decision D2 (FT2 gate intentionally drift-only/warn-only); day-0 consumer gate needs §3.5 soak. Prerequisites: crons fire ≥1× cleanly + ~7-14d soak + D2 reconsideration.

implementation.notes: Cross-repo: fitme-story #252 (consumer checker + audit-log sampler + weekly cron + warn-only CI + fresh vendored fixture) + FT2 #790 (re-sample stale fixtures). Verified before building that the gate-coverage shape check already existed (PR #209) — avoided duplication.

testing.notes: 16/16 new tests pass (7 checker + 9 sampler); gate-coverage-aggregator 9/9; npm run contract:check → PASS; workflow YAML valid. FT2 post-merge: integrity-check 0 findings, check-contract-fixtures all fresh.

docs.approval_signal: (post-merge closure batch — case study + backlog strike shipped in feature PR)

transitions.reason: PR #790 squashed onto main as 2cb09a9d58 (operator-merged 2026-06-22T06:31:43Z). Closure landed via close-feature.py. CI checks green; operator review preceded merge.
PR #790 squashed onto main as 2cb09a9d58 (operator-merged 2026-06-22T06:31:43Z). Closure landed via close-feature.py. Squash-merged.
Case study + backlog strike shipped in same feature PR. No additional docs.

Feature CLOSED via close-feature.py on chore/contract-fixture-consumer-adoption-post-merge-closure. Closes the testing→complete drift-pattern documented across 5+ instances by automating the post-merge audit trail.
